serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is one samosa piece weighing approximately 60 grams, providing around 186 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Cheesy Samosa Jalapeño — Singapore HPB Foodo Database (G00010374)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Foodo database record G00010374 for Cheesy Samosa Jalapeño. Saturated fat estimated from cheese and frying oil composition using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as a secondary reference for fatty acid breakdown, as HPB does not report saturated fat separately for this item.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; analogous food: deep-fried refined wheat flour pastry / samosa
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Cheesy Samosa Jalapeño. GI estimated at 70 (high) based on the closest analogous food: deep-fried refined refined wheat flour pastry (samosa/spring roll pastry shell), consistent with University of Sydney GI Database entries for fried refined flour pastry products (GI range 65–75). Glycemic load per 60g serving calculated as: (70 × 33.16 × 0.60) / 100 = 13.9g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 388 mg/100g with insufficient potassium (166 mg/100g) and fibre (1.8 g/100g) to offset cardiovascular sodium load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit intake of Cheesy Samosa Jalapeño due to borderline-high sodium at 388 mg/100g, compounded by low potassium (166 mg/100g, below the 200 mg/100g offset threshold) and low dietary fibre (1.8 g/100g), and should avoid additional high-sodium dipping sauces.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI refined wheat flour pastry (estimated GI 70) with 33.2 g/100g carbohydrate and only 1.8 g/100g dietary fibre, causing rapid postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly limit Cheesy Samosa Jalapeño because the deep-fried refined flour pastry shell has a high estimated GI of 70 and delivers 33.2 g carbohydrate per 100g with minimal fibre buffering, producing rapid postprandial glucose spikes; if consumed, limit to one piece and pair with a high-fibre salad.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat 17.47 g/100g including estimated saturated fat ~7.5 g/100g and trans fat ~0.2 g/100g from deep-frying, alongside sodium 388 mg/100g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid Cheesy Samosa Jalapeño due to its high total fat (17.47 g/100g), elevated saturated fat (~7.5 g/100g from cheese exceeding the 5 g/100g CVD threshold), estimated trans fat (~0.2 g/100g from frying oil), and borderline-high sodium (388 mg/100g), all of which adversely affect lipid profiles and blood pressure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates pro-inflammatory AGEs; estimated trans fat ~0.2 g/100g; saturated fat ~7.5 g/100g; jalapeño is a nightshade vegetable
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Cheesy Samosa Jalapeño because deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that worsen joint inflammation, the estimated trans fat (~0.2 g/100g) is a hard pro-inflammatory trigger, saturated fat from cheese (~7.5 g/100g) exceeds the 5 g/100g RA threshold, and jalapeño as a nightshade vegetable may exacerbate symptoms in sensitive individuals.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Cheesy Samosa Jalapeño is a deep-fried triangular pastry snack popular in Singapore, consisting of a crispy refined wheat flour shell filled with a spiced cheese and jalapeño pepper mixture. The pastry is golden-brown and crunchy on the outside, with a rich, creamy, mildly spicy interior from the melted cheese and sliced or diced jalapeño. It is typically served as a snack, appetiser, or light meal accompaniment. Nutritionally, per 100g it provides 310 kcal, 17.47g fat (including approximately 7.5g saturated fat from cheese and an estimated 0.2g trans fat from frying oil), 33.16g carbohydrate, 5.14g protein, 1.8g dietary fibre, and 388mg sodium. The high refined carbohydrate content and deep-fried preparation give it a high estimated glycemic index (~70), making it unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The jalapeño component, a nightshade vegetable, may also be a concern for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis. This item is classified as a processed fried snack and is not suitable for vegan or gluten-free diets, but is generally considered halal and vegetarian.

Saturated fat estimated from cheese and frying oil composition using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as a secondary reference. GI estimated from closest analogous food (deep-fried refined wheat flour pastry) from University of Sydney GI Database. Trans fat estimated from deep-frying context consistent with published literature.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this specific fried pastry product is not reported in the HPB Foodo database (G00010374) or USDA FoodData Central for an equivalent cheesy jalapeño samosa as of 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB source for this item. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient panel not reported in HPB Foodo for this item and no equivalent approved-source entry provides complete micronutrient data for this specific product. trans_fat_g set to 0.2 (estimated): deep-frying in vegetable oil typically generates trace trans fats; this is an estimate consistent with published data for similar fried pastry products and is documented in the health_context. saturated_fat_g set to 7.5 (estimated): derived from cheese filling composition using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cheddar cheese data as a secondary reference, as HPB does not report saturated fat separately for this item. Food group corrected from input 'Meat and alternatives / Beef' to 'Snacks / Fried pastry snacks' as this is a vegetarian cheese-filled fried pastry with no beef content. Primary cuisine corrected from 'Middle Eastern' to 'South Asian / Singaporean fusion' as samosa is a South Asian in origin, adapted in the Singapore food context.
